name: RustBelt λRust
summary: RustBelt's λRust (Jung et al., POPL 2018) is a formal IR for a Rust subset, defined in Coq. λRust is the substrate for soundness proofs — every Rust unsafe library (Arc, Rc, Cell, RefCell, Mutex) gets a soundness statement in λRust, proven via lifetime logic.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: RustBelt's λRust (Jung et al., POPL 2018) is a formal IR for Rust subset, defined in Coq. λRust is the substrate for soundness proofs — every Rust unsafe library (Arc, Rc, Cell, RefCell, Mutex) gets a soundness statement in λRust, proven via lifetime logic. The IR is mechanized: every operation has a small-step semantics in Coq, and proofs are checked machine-by-machine.

sections.title: IR-as-formal-artifact
sections.content:
This is the IR-as-formal-artifact extreme: λRust is not compiled and run; it's defined and reasoned about. Yet it counts as a language representation because it captures what Rust *means* mathematically, and every other Rust-related verifier (Verus, Prusti, Creusot, Aeneas, RefinedRust) builds atop or adjacent to it.

This record is the canonical formal-soundness model for Rust's aliasing discipline (λRust + lifetime logic, mechanised in Coq via `data/iris-separation-logic`); other Rust-targeting verifiers — `data/verus`, `data/prusti`, `data/creusot`, `data/aeneas-verifier`, `data/refinedrust` — build on or adjacent to it.
